title: ficus apiocarpa
intro: Ficus apiocarpa, also known as the Indian banyan, is a species of fig tree native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ficus apiocarpa include reducing fever, alleviating cough and cold symptoms, and treating digestive problems. Ficus apiocarpa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its leaves and bark can also be used to make topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
